chunks.text: Technical Reports - 1965. Constrained Minimization Problems In Finite Dimensional Spaces (M149) by M. Canon, C. Cullum and E. Polak. Normal Form and Stability of a Class of Coupled Nonlinear Networks (M141) by P.P. Varaiya and R-W Liu. An Algorithm for Reducing a Linear, Time-Invariant Differential System to State Form (M140) by E. Polak. A Frequency-Domain Stability Criterion for Feedback Systems Containing a Single Linear Time-Varying Element (M139) by Y.L. Kuo. The Minimal Realization of a Nonanticipative Impulse Response Matrix (M138) by C.A. Desoer and P. Varaiya. Nonlinear Programming in Banach Space (M137) by P.P. Varaiya. The Spatial Distribution of Secondary Electron Emission (M136) by R.F.W. Pease. Some Results Concerning the Zero-Crossings of Gaussian Noise (M135) by E. Wong. Ultrasonic Surface-Wave Amplification In Cadmium Sulfide (M134) by R.M. White and F.W. Voltmer. On the Stability of Feedback Systems With One Differentiable Nonlinear Element (M133) by A.G. Dewey.
chunks.question: What are some technical reports from 1965 at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Reports on minimization, networks, and stability.

chunks.text: Direct Piezoelectric Coupling to Surface Elastic Waves (M132) by R.M. White and F.W. Voltmer. Optimum Locations on a Graph With Probabilistic Demands (M131) by H. Frank. Direct Measurement of the Depletion Layer Width Variation vs. Applied Bias for a Pin Junction (M130) by N.C. MacDonald and T.E. Everhart. Nonlinear Programming and Optimal Control (M129) by Pravin P. Varaiya. The Minimal Realization of Impulse Response Matrices (M128) by C.A. Desoer. A Deflection-Modulation Cathode-Ray-Tube Display (M127) by T.E. Everhart. Absolute Intensity of Synchrotron Radiation Spectrum From a Hot Electron Plasma (M126) by A.J. Lichtenberg, S. Sesnic and A.W. Trivelpiece. Self-Organizing Control Systems: The Application of Pattern Recognition in Learning Systems (M125) by Ralph P. Iwens. A Boundary Layer Model for Velocity Space Instabilities (M124) by J.S. Jayson and A.J. Lichtenberg. Some Sufficient Conditions for Continuous Linear-Programming (M123) by A. Larsen and E. Polak.
chunks.question: What topics do the 1965 technical reports cover?
chunks.answer: Piezoelectric coupling, graph optimization, and control systems.

chunks.text: Theoretical Investigation of a Non-planar Field Effect Transistor (M122) by S.R. Levine. Surface Mobility of Silicon MOS Devices (M120) by T.I. Kamins. Theory of Optimum Discrete Time Systems (M119) by H. Halkin, B.W. Jordan, E. Polak and J.B. Rosen. Absolute Radiation Standard in the Far Infrared (M118) by A. J. Lichtenberg and S. Sesnic. Optical Photon-Acoustic Phonon Interactions; Stimulated Brillouin Scattering (M117) by A. Pine. A Note on Alzerman's Conjecture (M115) by A.G. Dewey and E.I. Jury. A Stability Inequality For a Class of Nonlinear Feedback Systems (M114) by A.G. Dewey. Processing of Telemetry Data Generated By Sensors Moving in a Varying Field (M113) by D.J. Sakrison. Nonlinear Sampled-Data Systems and Multidimensional Z-Transform (M112) by A. Rault and E.I. Jury. On the Stability of Nonlinear Sampled-Data Feedback Systems (M109) by C.T. Chen. On Convergence of the Solutions of Differential Equations Involving Brownian Motion (M65/5) by Eugene Wong.
chunks.question: What are key research areas in the 1965 technical reports?
chunks.answer: Transistors, discrete systems, and stability analysis.
